summary: High‑energy second set sandwich, stellar guitar and tight rhythm
blurb: The second set shines with a flawless The Other One → Sitting On Top Of The World sandwich, soaring Not Fade Away and China Cat riffs. Jerry delivers perfect guitar tone while Keith’s sweet piano adds fresh color, and Bill’s drumming locks the jams in place. Highlights include Good Lovin’, Brokedown Palace, Bertha and Big Railroad Blues.
review: December 10‑1971 at St. Louis’ Fox Theatre captures a band on the cusp of a new era. The first set rolls through classic crowd‑pleasers – Bertha, Tennessee Jed and an exuberant Big Railroad Blues – giving a vivid snapshot of the emerging Keith Godchaux/Pigpen lineup. But it’s the second set that cements this show in the vaults: a monster The Other One → Sitting On Top Of The World sandwich builds from Truckin’ into a soaring climax with Not Fade Away, China Cat riffs and a jubilant encore. Jerry’s guitar tone is described as “perfect,” delivering powerful solos that drive each jam, while Phil’s bass provides rock‑solid footing. Bob steps forward vocally on NFA, Keith’s early piano/organ work is sweet and instantly integrated, and Bill’s drumming offers “perfect support” with a post‑drums transition on The Other One hailed as one of the best ever. The soundboard capture (gd71-12-10.sbd.yerys.1311.sbeok.shnf) balances clarity with the theater’s natural ambience, making this performance both historically significant and an absolute listening pleasure.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across recordings: two sources call the show 'high‑energy' and 'blistering,' praising the The Other One sandwich, Good Lovin', and Keith's piano. Only one mixed review notes minor track splits, but no negative criticism of performance appears. Consistent accolades for guitar tone, bass support, and drumming justify a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Most complete capture (includes the acclaimed second‑set sandwich) with good‑balanced soundboard mix and sweet theater ambience.

key_highlights: Monster The Other One → Sitting On Top Of The World sandwich that builds into a soaring finish.
Blistering Good Lovin’ and unforgettable Brokedown Palace in the high‑energy second set.
Keith Godchaux’s early piano/organ tone is sweet and instantly integrated, adding fresh color.
High‑quality soundboard capture balances clarity with theater ambience.
song_highlights: Sitting On Top Of The World

band_performance.Jerry: Perfect guitar tone, leading the band into new material with powerful solos throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides perfect bass support, anchoring the high‑energy jams and keeping the groove tight.
band_performance.Bob: Emerges vocally backing Pig on Not Fade Away and delivers strong rhythm work.
band_performance.Keith: Early piano/organ tone is sweet, quickly integrating into the group and adding fresh color to the mix.
band_performance.Billy: Perfect support on drums; his post‑drums transition during The Other One is hailed as one of the best ever.

must_listen_sequences: Sitting On Top Of The World
Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
